Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add adversarial tests for ownership analysis pass semantics

Lock in correctness properties for insert_releases_for_dead_params and
insert_retains_for_reused_call_args with 12 new tests:

HIR-based tests verify the full pipeline:
- @fip functions are excluded from Release insertion
- Unboxed records are excluded (stack-only, no RC)
- Sum params with managed/Dynamic payload skip Case 2 (double-free guard)
- Record params returned directly have no Release

Direct MirFunction tests exercise the pass internals:
- Retain inserted before call when value reused after
- No Retain when value is not reused
- Case 2 fires for single-block record param (field load → Release)
- Case 1 fires for dead branch block (Release at head of dead successor)
- No Release when param is consumed by Call (ownership transferred)
- Case 2 fires for sum param with primitive (Int) payload
- Case 2 does NOT fire for sum param with Dynamic payload
- Case 1 does NOT fire for sum-typed params (unsafe without Perceus RC=1)

Also fix is_heap_managed_type for Sum types: use `any variant has fields`
instead of `all variants have fields`. The old check was designed for codegen
layout decisions, not RC safety. A List with Nil/Cons IS heap-managed because
Cons stores a counted reference; releasing the outer sum after payload
extraction would double-free the inner pointer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix webhook upsert failure: replace upsert with insert-or-update

Migration 00024 replaced the simple UNIQUE constraint on user_id with a
partial unique index (WHERE status NOT IN ('canceled','unpaid')), which
PostgreSQL cannot use for ON CONFLICT. Switch to explicit select →
insert/update pattern. Add temporary debug logs for webhook events.

Add appProtocol field to Holmes service port configuration (#1666)

## Summary
Added the `appProtocol` field to the Holmes service port specification
in the Helm chart to explicitly declare the application protocol as
HTTP.

## Key Changes
- Added `appProtocol: http` to the service port definition in
`helm/holmes/templates/holmes.yaml`

## Implementation Details
This change improves Kubernetes service discovery and networking by
explicitly declaring that the service port uses the HTTP protocol. The
`appProtocol` field helps Kubernetes and service mesh implementations
(like Istio) better understand the protocol being used on this port,
enabling more intelligent traffic management and observability features.

https://claude.ai/code/session_015XXWNF93Vn1kfMQ4wyHqsY

feat: iterative consensus, configurable council, expert roles, metrics & export

- Replace ranking-based Stage 2 with iterative consensus loop
  - Rounds 1-3: unanimous agreement required
  - Rounds 4-5: 2/3 majority required
  - After 5 rounds: chairman decides
- Add configurable council via Settings panel
  - Pick council models, chairman, consensus rules from UI
  - Settings persist to data/settings.json
  - 10 models available across OpenAI, Google, Anthropic
- Add expert roles system
  - Assign personas (Security Expert, UX Designer, etc.) per model
  - Custom roles supported
  - Roles shown as tags in Stage 1
- Add cost & latency dashboard
  - Track tokens, cost, latency per model per API call
  - Per-model breakdown table + summary chips
  - Approximate cost estimation using model pricing
- Add markdown export
  - Export full deliberation as formatted markdown
  - Includes all stages, rounds, votes, and final answer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 4: Cron worker processing logic with auth fallback

Pipeline endpoints (classify, enrich, score):
- Add cron-secret auth fallback: reads body first, then checks session OR
  x-cron-secret header. Session path tried first so instant mode is untouched.

Cron worker (pipeline-worker/route.ts):
- Add x-cron-secret header to all HTTP calls to pipeline endpoints
- Safe JSON parsing with callEndpoint() helper — handles deployment
  protection HTML responses gracefully

- Weighted ETA: classify=1x, enrich=3x, score=2x
- Admin actions: pause, cancel, restart (full reset), retry_failed,
  force_complete (now sends completion email)

commit.message:
feat: e2e header verification and UI test fixes (#46)

* feat: add e2e header verification tests with Bun test server

Add comprehensive end-to-end tests that verify the Chrome extension
actually applies header modifications to real HTTP requests, using
a Bun HTTP echo server and Playwright with extension loading.

Test coverage includes:
- Request headers: set, remove, append operations
- Response headers: set, remove, append operations
- Combined request + response operations
- Disabled headers and empty-name headers
- URL filter match types: host_equals, localhost_port,
  url_starts_with, path_starts_with, url_contains
- Include/exclude filter logic and precedence
- Profile switching
- Edge cases: special chars, case sensitivity, bulk headers,
  empty profiles, non-HTTP URLs, empty values
- Full UI pipeline: popup interaction through to network

Closes #45

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: filter out set/append headers with empty value to prevent rule rejection

Chrome's declarativeNetRequest API requires a value for set/append
operations. Previously, a header with operation 'set' and an empty
value would omit the value field, causing Chrome to silently reject
the entire rule — meaning all other valid headers in the profile
were also not applied.

Now set/append headers with empty values are filtered out during
rule building so they don't poison the rest of the rule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update e2e UI tests with correct selectors and optimized config

- Rewrite all e2e test selectors to match current UI (inline action
  buttons instead of dropdown menus, updated class names, data-testids)
- Add data-testid="add-profile" and aria-label to ProfileSidebar
- Add timeout configuration to playwright.config.ts
- Use addInitScript for localStorage cleanup (eliminates double navigation)
- Unified test script: `vitest run && playwright test`
- Add watch: false to vitest config, exclude .claude/** from test runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: combine CI into single job and fix script references

- Merge unit and e2e jobs into one to avoid duplicate setup
- Fix test:run → test:unit script reference
- Run unit tests, UI e2e tests, then header verification e2e tests sequentially

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: align badge count with rule filtering and remove headless conflict

- getEnabledHeaderCount now excludes set/append headers with empty values,
  matching buildSessionRuleFromProfile so badge count reflects actual rules
- Use .trim() on value checks for consistency
- Align countAppliedHeadersForTab in tests with same filter
- Remove --headless=new arg that conflicted with headless: false in fixtures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings across background, config, and security

- Fix clearDynamicRulesOnce race condition with promise caching pattern
- Remove redundant onInstalled initialize() call that caused double init
- Add content_security_policy to manifest.json for defense-in-depth
- Fix type weakening in App.vue (Record<string, unknown> → Partial<HeaderRule>)
- Gate VueMcp plugin to development mode only in vite.config.ts
- Add webServer block to e2e playwright config for reliable server lifecycle
- Simplify CI workflow by removing manual test server management

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Oskar Nilsen Roos <oskar.roos@mecenat.com>

commit.message:
FIX: Override .glass margin-bottom causing box misalignment

ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED: The .glass class in globals.css has margin-bottom
defined in multiple places (1rem on mobile, 1.5rem/2rem on various breakpoints).
This margin was pushing one box down, creating the "drooping" effect.

Solution: Added `!mb-0` (margin-bottom: 0 !important) to both Key Strengths
and Risk Factors boxes to override the global .glass margin.

Why previous attempts failed:
- Grid and flexbox layouts were working correctly
- The issue was OUTSIDE the layout system - margin on the elements themselves
- The margin-bottom from .glass was applied AFTER the grid positioned the elements

This fix ensures both boxes sit at the exact same vertical position with no
additional margin pushing one down.

Location: components/valuation/ValuationResults.tsx:251,273

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
